{"author": null, "closed_at": null, "collaborating_teams": null, "collaborating_users": null, "created_at": null, "credits": [], "credits_detailed": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-33658", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}}, "cwe_ids": [], "cwes": [], "description": "### Impact
Active Storage's proxy controller does not limit the number of byte ranges in an HTTP Range header. A request with thousands of small ranges causes disproportionate CPU usage compared to a normal request for the same file, possibly resulting in a DoS vulnerability.

### Releases
The fixed releases are available at the normal locations.

### Credit
This issue was responsibly reported by Hackerone researcher [thwin_htet](https://hackerone.com/thwin_htet).", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-p9fm-f462-ggrg", "html_url": title: Possible DoS vulnerability in Active Storage proxy mode via multi-range requests
